2605.02740.title: Foundation Models to Unlock Real-World Evidence from Nationwide Medical Claims
2605.02740.paper_summary: Evidence derived from large-scale real-world data (RWD) is increasingly informing regulatory evaluation and healthcare decision-making. Administrative claims provide population-scale, longitudinal records of healthcare utilization, expenditure, and detailed coding of diagnoses, procedures, and medications, yet their potential as a substrate for healthcare foundation models remains largely unexplored. Here we present ReClaim, a generative transformer trained from scratch on 43.8 billion medical events from more than 200 million enrollees in the MarketScan claims data spanning 2008-2022. ReClaim models longitudinal trajectories across diagnoses, procedures, medications, and expenditure, and was scaled to 140 million, 700 million, and 1.7 billion parameters. Across over 1,000 disease-onset prediction tasks, ReClaim achieved a mean AUC of 75.6%, substantially outperforming disease-specific LightGBM (66.3%) and the transformer-based Delphi model (69.4%), with the largest gains for rare diseases. These advantages held across retrospective and prospective evaluations and in external validation on two independent datasets. Performance improved monotonically with scale, and post-training added 13.8 percentage points over pre-training alone. Beyond disease prediction, ReClaim captured financial outcomes and improved real-world evidence (RWE) analyses: for healthcare expenditure forecasting it increased explained variance from 0.28 to 0.37 relative to LightGBM, and in a target trial emulation it reduced systematic bias by 72% on average relative to Delphi. Together, these results establish administrative claims as a scalable substrate for healthcare foundation models and show that learned representations generalize across time periods and data sources, supporting disease surveillance, expenditure forecasting, and RWE generation.
2605.02740.paper_summary_zh: 來自大規模現實世界數據（RWD）的證據越來越多地影響著監管評估和醫療決策。行政索賠提供了人口規模的、縱向的醫療利用、支出記錄，以及診斷、程序和藥物的詳細編碼，但作為醫療基礎模型的基礎，其潛力仍然大多未被探索。在此，我們介紹 ReClaim，一個從頭開始訓練的生成型Transformer，基於 2008-2022 年 MarketScan 索賠數據中超過 2 億名參與者的 438 億醫療事件。ReClaim 模型捕捉了診斷、程序、藥物和支出的縱向軌跡，並擴展到 1.4 億、7 億和 17 億個參數。在超過 1,000 個疾病發作預測任務中，ReClaim 的平均 AUC 為 75.6%，顯著超越了特定疾病的 LightGBM（66.3%）和基於Transformer的 Delphi 模型（69.4%），對於罕見疾病的增益最大。這些優勢在回顧性和前瞻性評估中以及在兩個獨立數據集上的外部驗證中均得以保持。隨著規模的增長，性能單調提高，訓練後相比僅預訓練增加了 13.8 個百分點。除了疾病預測，ReClaim 還捕捉了財務結果並改善了現實世界證據（RWE）分析：在醫療支出預測中，它相對於 LightGBM 將解釋變異提高了從 0.28 到 0.37，而在目標試驗模擬中，平均減少了 72% 的系統性偏差。總之，這些結果確立了行政索賠作為醫療基礎模型的可擴展基礎，並顯示學習到的表示在不同時間段和數據源之間具有泛化能力，支持疾病監測、支出預測和 RWE 生成。
2605.02740.author: Fan Ma et.al.
2605.02740.authors: Fan Ma, Yuntian Liu, Xiang Lan, Weipeng Zhou, Jun Ni, Mauro Giuffrè, Lingfei Qian, Xueqing Peng, Yujia Zhou, Ruey-Ling Weng, Huan He, Lu Li, Qingyu Chen, Andrew Loza, Laila Rasmy, Degui Zhi, Yuan Lu, Chenjie Zeng, Joshua C Denny, Lee Schwamm, Daniella Meeker, Lucila Ohno-Machado, Yong Chen, Hua Xu
